title: calophyllum ferrugineum
intro: Calophyllum ferrugineum, also known as the ironwood tree, is a plant species native to the tropical rainforests of Malaysia and Indonesia, belonging to the family Calophyllaceae. The medicinal properties of Calophyllum ferrugineum lie in its bark, which contains calophyllolide, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. The herbal preparations of Calophyllum ferrugineum include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
